name: azure-search-documents-ts
description: Build search applications using Azure AI Search SDK for JavaScript (@azure/search-documents). Use when creating/managing indexes, implementing vector/hybrid search, semantic ranking, or building agentic retrieval with knowledge bases.
capabilities: Create and manage Azure Search indexes with vector field configurations
Upload, merge, and delete documents in batch operations
Perform full-text search with filtering and faceting
Implement vector similarity search using embeddings
Combine text and vector search for hybrid results
Enable semantic ranking and extractive answers
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation for Azure AI Search SDK with TypeScript code examples. All static analyzer findings are false positives caused by misinterpreting markdown code fences. The skill reads environment variables for configuration, which is standard practice for Azure SDK authentication.
valueStatement: Implement powerful search capabilities with Azure AI Search SDK. Create vector indexes, hybrid search, and semantic ranking for intelligent document retrieval.
limitations: Requires Azure Search service subscription and endpoint configuration
Vector embeddings must be generated by external embedding service
Semantic search requires Azure Search tier that supports it
Authentication requires Azure credentials or API keys

useCases.title: Product Search with Semantic Ranking
useCases.description: Build an e-commerce search that combines text matching with vector similarity for relevant product discovery.

useCases.title: Document Retrieval System
useCases.description: Create a knowledge base search with hybrid search for finding relevant documents by meaning and keywords.

useCases.title: Autocomplete and Suggestions
useCases.description: Implement search-as-you-type with Azure Search suggesters for improved user experience.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Index Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Azure Search index for a products catalog with fields for id, title, description, category, and price. Make title and description searchable.

promptTemplates.title: Vector Search Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Add vector search to my Azure Search index. I have 1536-dimensional embeddings from Azure OpenAI. Show me how to configure the vector field and perform similarity search.

promptTemplates.title: Hybrid Search Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement hybrid search that combines keyword matching with vector similarity. Show me how to tune the results and rank by relevance.

promptTemplates.title: Semantic Search with Answers
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure semantic search on my Azure Search index to return extractive answers and captions. Explain the semantic configuration and query options needed.

bestPractices: Use hybrid search combining text and vector for best relevance across query types
Enable semantic ranking to improve results for natural language queries
Batch document operations using uploadDocuments instead of single document calls
antiPatterns: Do not upload documents one at a time - use batch operations for efficiency
Avoid using includeTotalCount in production queries as it impacts performance
Do not skip index field configuration - properly mark searchable, filterable, and facetable fields

faq.question: What Azure Search tier do I need for vector search?
faq.answer: Vector search requires Azure Search Basic tier or higher. Semantic search requires Standard tier or higher.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Search?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production with managed identity, or admin/query API keys for development. Store credentials in environment variables.

faq.question: What embedding models work with Azure Search vector fields?
faq.answer: Any embedding model that produces numeric vectors. Azure OpenAI embeddings (text-embedding-ada-002) produce 1536-dimensional vectors commonly used with Azure Search.

faq.question: How do I update existing documents in the index?
faq.answer: Use mergeOrUploadDocuments with the document key. Azure Search merges fields you provide and preserves existing values for unspecified fields.

faq.question: Can I filter vector search results?
faq.answer: Yes, combine vectorSearchOptions with filter parameter. Filtering happens before vector search, reducing the candidate set for similarity matching.

faq.question: What is the difference between semantic and vector search?
faq.answer: Vector search finds similar content using embeddings. Semantic search uses AI to understand query intent, extract answers, and provide captions. They can be used together for best results.
